human_verification.notes: Reviewed validated readiness for facilities work-order execution, permit and inspection boundaries, downtime and return-to-service handling, functionality coverage, and runtime-specification-based posture.

specialist_owned_evidence: Manifest enforces facilities-specific execution boundaries for work-order progression, exception handling, completion evidence, downtime coordination, and return-to-service posture with explicit routing to project controls, permitting, inspection, safety, superintendent, and licensed trade lanes.
Authoritative sources cover work-order workflow, permit and inspection gating, safety blockers, subcontractor and submittal evidence, and closeout requirements rather than only one dimension.
Functionality map covers all required shared, execution-processing-and-fulfillment, and construction-and-field-services abilities with scenario coverage for every scenario in evaluation results.
Research summary maps workflow stages, artifacts, systems of record, decision boundaries, domain failure modes, and required scenario families to this facilities execution lane.

review_notes: No functional or evidence blockers remain for this package draft.

All required scenario families and escalation families are represented, including permit or site-readiness gaps, closeout blockers, licensed-trade boundary refusal, final completion refusal, and tenant adaptation.

notes: validated conditions met for recommendation-only facilities work-order execution specialist.
Runtime remains specification-based and must be promoted to a retained implementation only through separate governance and commit history.
